meta.purpose: Sanitize manhwa-recap narration before TTS, and screen titles/tags/descriptions, against YouTube advertiser-friendly risk.

actions.replace: Auto-swap a grammatically-safe token. Applied silently. Use ONLY where the swap fits the same slot in any voice/tense.
actions.flag: Do NOT auto-rewrite. Surface for a regenerate/review pass. Used where a dumb swap would break grammar or where framing matters.
actions.block: Must not publish as-is. Halt the pipeline. Used for slurs and sexual violence that need a human/LLM rewrite to implication-level.

scopes.metadata: Applies to title / thumbnail text / tags / description (stricter).

notes: 'damn', 'hell', 'crap' are generally advertiser-safe and are intentionally NOT listed.
Slurs are intentionally NOT enumerated here. Point slur_list_file at your own maintained newline-separated list; every term in it is treated as a hard block.
Replacement lists rotate per occurrence (seeded by video id) so narration doesn't repeat the same euphemism.
FIX vs original: sexual_violence 'forced himself/herself' tightened to require on/onto/upon so 'forced himself to stand' is NOT a false hard-block.

profanity.note: Gendered insult. Paraphrase in narration ('she snapped at her', 'the cruel remark') rather than voicing it.

violence.note: Soften to 'brutalized', 'made him suffer', or imply off-screen. Don't dwell on method.
Graphic detail is the main demonetization risk in recaps. Cut the visual; keep only the consequence ('he didn't survive the fight').
Intensity term. Reframe to scale without the loaded word ('the whole clan was wiped out').

Ambiguous between physical and sexual assault. Check context: if sexual, treat as sexual_violence (block/rewrite); if physical, soften ('attacked', 'beat'). NOTE: noisy in action manhwa ('assault on the fortress'); the generator-side rules should pre-soften so this rarely fires.

sexual.note: Grammar-sensitive; don't token-swap. Rewrite to imply: 'they slept together', 'spent the night', 'were intimate'.
Paraphrase the insult in narration; don't voice it.

HARD BLOCK. Never narrate explicitly. Rewrite to implication only ('he crossed a line he never should have', 'violated her trust') or cut the beat. Publishing with this in the transcript risks the whole video. FIX: requires on/onto/upon so 'forced himself to stand' is not a false block.
HARD BLOCK in narration/metadata. Rewrite around it.

substance.note: Overlaps self-harm. Frame carefully or imply ('took too much and collapsed').

self_harm_suicide.note: SENSITIVE - do not auto-swap and do not sensationalize. Can stay monetized with careful, non-graphic framing. Prefer 'ended her own life', 'couldn't go on', 'chose to leave this world'. Route every hit through manual/LLM framing review; never name a method.
